case_memory.chief_complaint: Planned admission for Cycle 4 of DA-EPOCH
current_turn_information: My symptoms started with lower extremity swelling and pulmonary congestion during my first chemotherapy session.
Since then, I’ve responded well to treatment, and my symptoms haven’t been progressively worsening.
I do have peripheral neuropathy since the beginning of treatment, but nothing else has gotten worse.
Today, I’m not having chest pain, shortness of breath, or other major issues — just some rhinorrhea and a trace cough this morning.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 50-year-old male, admitted for Cycle 4 of DA-EPOCH. History: high-grade B-cell lymphoma (stage III), hypertension, pulmonary hypertension, diabetes. Past chemo tolerated well; prior lower extremity swelling and pulmonary congestion; peripheral neuropathy since start of treatment. Today: denies chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, chills; notes rhinorrhea and trace cough. Physical: admission vitals 98.1, 122/68, 100, 20, 97% RA; no lower extremity swelling; discharge vitals 97.8, 150-180/96-104, 65-91, 18, 97-98% RA; 2+ pitting edema bilaterally. Port at right subclavian intact. Final result: Encounter for antineoplastic chemotherapy.
